auto_posts.title: Azure Local Update — February 5, 2026
auto_posts.subtitle: Documentation changes
auto_posts.content:
# Azure Local Documentation Updates: Key Changes for Operations Teams

Microsoft updated Azure Local deployment documentation with critical terminology changes. The SQL Server deployment guide now explicitly lists supported workloads: "Online transaction processing (OLTP) workloads" replaces the previous generic description. VM prerequisite documentation changed "Access to an Azure subscription" to "Access to an Azure subscription with the appropriate Azure role-based access control (RBAC) role." This clarifies that default subscription access isn't sufficient—teams must verify RBAC permissions before attempting deployments.

**Action Required:** Review your team's Azure RBAC assignments against the updated requirements in `/manage/assign-vm-rbac-roles.md`. The AKS cluster creation title changed from "How to deploy" to "Deploy," but the underlying process remains unchanged. Update any internal runbooks referencing these document titles for consistency.

Updated documentation: aks-create-clusters-portal • sql-server-23h2 • hci-vm-prerequisites • hci-vm-sign-in-set-subscription • create-arc-virtual-machines • whats-new
